subtitle: A shallow family and beginner-windsurf bay in Apokoronas, Crete, whose eastern edge holds the open-air mosaic floor of a three-aisled 6th-century Christian basilica.
spike_statement: A calm, shallow windsurf bay whose edge holds the open-air mosaic floor of a three-aisled 6th-century basilica, excavated 1974-1989.

levers.photo_tone: Flat, shallow turquoise bay backed by the White Mountains; a few steps inland the roofless, fenced mosaic floor of the basilica lies exposed to the sky, its tesserae pale gold and grey against the sand
